title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful, this Indian favorite is easier than you think! Get the step-by-step guide.
longTailKeywords: best Butter Chicken recipe at home
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a side of naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy goodness of homemade Classic Butter Chicken, a true Indian culinary delight.

description: Experience the rich and creamy delight of Classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy. Infused with aromatic spices and finished with butter and cream, this Murgh Makhani recipe promises an authentic taste of India, perfect for a comforting meal.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook in batches until lightly browned on all sides and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until aromatic.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato and Cashew Puree
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Bring to a gentle simmer. Add the soaked and drained cashews. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides. Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods if desired.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the gravy mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water to achieve the desired consistency. Pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth, restaurant-style texture. This step is optional but highly recommended.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the smooth gravy. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, sugar, and crushed kasuri methi. Stir well and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Finally, stir in the heavy cream and cook for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the sauce does not boil vigorously after adding cream. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer the marination, the more tender and flavorful the chicken will be.
Using good quality canned crushed tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes is key for the sauce's flavor. Blending cashews into the gravy gives it a rich, creamy texture without excessive cream.
Serve Butter Chicken hot with fresh naan bread, roti, or steamed basmati rice. A sprinkle of fresh cilantro and a swirl of cream make for a beautiful presentation.
article:
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani: A Culinary Journey into Indian Flavors

Few dishes evoke the warmth, comfort, and rich tapestry of Indian cuisine quite like Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. This iconic dish has transcended its origins in Delhi, India, to become a global favorite, cherished for its incredibly tender chicken bathed in a velvety, subtly sweet, and tangy tomato-based sauce. If you’ve ever wondered about the magic behind this beloved Indian chicken recipe, you’re in the right place. We’ll delve into its history, essential ingredients, step-by-step preparation, and tips to perfect your own classic Butter Chicken at home.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its story begins in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, where Kundan Lal Gujral, one of the founders, is credited with its creation. The tale goes that Gujral, needing a way to use leftover tandoori chicken, simmered it in a rich tomato gravy enriched with butter and cream. The result was a revelation, and thus, one of the world's most famous curries was born.

At its heart, Butter Chicken is characterized by succulent pieces of chicken (often tandoor-cooked or grilled) swimming in a creamy, mildly spiced tomato sauce. The sauce's distinctive flavor comes from a careful balance of tomatoes, butter, cream, and a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek (kasuri methi), ginger, and garlic. Unlike some fiery Indian curries, Murgh Makhani is known for its gentle heat and comforting, luxurious texture, making it appealing to a wide range of palates. It’s a dish that embodies indulgence and culinary craftsmanship.

## Ingredients for Classic Butter Chicken

Creating an authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s what you’ll typically need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   **Chicken**: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces. Thighs are often preferred for their tenderness.
*   **Yogurt**: Plain full-fat yogurt, which tenderizes the chicken and helps spices adhere.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste**: Freshly ground for maximum flavor.
*   **Spices**: Red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color without excessive heat), turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala.
*   **Lemon Juice**: Adds tang and aids in tenderizing.

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   **Tomatoes**: Fresh ripe tomatoes, pureed, or good quality canned crushed tomatoes. They form the backbone of the sauce.
*   **Onions**: Finely chopped or pureed, adding sweetness and body.
*   **Butter**: Unsalted butter is crucial for its namesake richness.
*   **Cream**: Heavy cream or full-fat coconut cream for that signature velvety texture.
*   **Cashews or Almonds**: Soaked and ground into a paste, they contribute to the sauce's creaminess and thickness.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste**: Again, for aromatic depth.
*   **Spices**: Garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, bay leaf, and a pinch of sugar to balance the acidity of the tomatoes.
*   **Oil**: For sautéing.
*   **Salt**: To taste.

## How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step

Making classic Butter Chicken is a labor of love, but the steps are straightforward and rewarding.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken**: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and all the marinade spices. Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best results.

2.  **Cook the Chicken**: Heat a little oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook in batches until browned on all sides and cooked through. Set aside. Traditionally, this is done in a tandoor, but pan-frying or grilling works perfectly.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base**: In the same pan, melt a generous amount of butter. Add whole spices like bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and green cardamom pods. Sauté for a minute until fragrant. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté until the raw smell disappears.

4.  **Add Onions and Tomatoes**: Add finely chopped or pureed onions and cook until translucent. Stir in the remaining powdered spices (turmeric, chili powder, cumin, coriander). Cook for a minute, then add the tomato puree. Bring to a simmer, add a pinch of sugar, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil separates from the sides (about 15-20 minutes). This step is crucial for developing a deep flavor.

5.  **Achieve Creaminess**: Stir in the cashew or almond paste (made by soaking nuts in hot water for 15 minutes, then blending with a little water until smooth). Cook for another 5 minutes. For an extra smooth sauce, you can now blend the entire sauce using an immersion blender or cool slightly and blend in a regular blender, then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This step ensures the signature silky texture of Butter Chicken.

6.  **Combine and Finish**: Return the smooth sauce to the pan. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir well to coat the chicken. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce.

7.  **Final Touches**: Stir in the heavy cream and crushed fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi). Heat gently for a couple of minutes – do not boil vigorously after adding cream. Check for seasoning and adjust salt if needed. Garnish with fresh coriander.

## Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

*   **Marination is Key**: Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be.
*   **Quality Ingredients**: Use ripe, flavorful tomatoes and fresh spices. Kashmiri chili powder offers a beautiful red hue without excessive heat.
*   **Slow Cook the Sauce**: Allow the tomato base to cook down slowly until the oil separates. This concentrates the flavors and removes any raw tomato taste.
*   **Blend for Smoothness**: For an authentic, restaurant-quality texture, blend the sauce until completely smooth. Straining it through a sieve after blending can remove any remaining fibrous bits, ensuring a truly silky finish for your Butter Chicken.
*   **Kasuri Methi**: This dried fenugreek leaf is non-negotiable for authentic Murgh Makhani flavor. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma.
*   **Balance Flavors**: Taste and adjust. A pinch of sugar balances tomato acidity, while more salt or a touch of lemon juice can brighten the flavors.
*   **Don't Overcook Chicken**: Cook the chicken just until done before adding it to the sauce, as it will continue to cook slightly in the gravy.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments. The most classic pairing is with warm, fluffy **Naan bread** or **Garlic Naan**, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. **Basmati rice**, whether plain or fragrant pilaf, is another excellent choice, providing a neutral canvas for the intense flavors of the curry.

You can also serve it with:
*   **Roti or Chapati**: Simpler Indian flatbreads.
*   **Cucumber Raita**: A cooling yogurt dip with cucumber and spices, which provides a lovely contrast to the richness of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Fresh Salad**: A simple green salad with a light vinaigrette can cut through the richness.
*   **Pickles or Chutneys**: For an extra burst of flavor and tang.

Garnish your Murgh Makhani with a swirl of fresh cream, a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro (coriander), or a few slivers of ginger for visual appeal and an added fresh note.

## Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is beloved, it’s also quite adaptable:

*   **Healthier Options**: Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute heavy cream with lighter cream or even cashew milk for a dairy-free version. You can also grill or bake the chicken instead of pan-frying.
*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani**: Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, chickpeas, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas for a delicious vegetarian "Butter Paneer" or "Butter Tofu."
*   **Spice Level**: Adjust the amount of chili powder to suit your preference. For more heat, add a fresh green chili or a pinch of cayenne pepper. For less, reduce the chili powder and focus on the milder Kashmiri variety.
*   **Smoky Flavor**: If you have access to a charcoal grill or tandoor, cooking the chicken this way will impart an authentic smoky flavor that elevates the dish significantly. A small piece of charcoal smoked in a bowl of ghee in the finished curry can also impart a subtle smoky aroma.
*   **Nut-Free**: If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews/almonds and rely on a little extra cream or a spoonful of cornstarch slurry (mixed with water) to thicken the sauce.

Butter Chicken is more than just a meal; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, balanced spices, and tender chicken make it a dish that delights the senses and offers a taste of India's rich culinary heritage. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook, mastering this classic Murgh Makhani will undoubtedly earn you rave reviews. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to embark on a truly delicious journey.
